reasoning: The composited asset (white fluffy poof sprite) is clearly spottable against the medium-dark dungeon stone floor — high luminance contrast makes it visible in under 0.5s and the silhouette is rounded and distinct from the rectangular tile pattern. Pixel-art style matches the game's native aesthetic and the bright, clean look is consistent with Echoes feel. However, two concerns push it to marginal: (1) the form reads as a character/entity rather than a transient VFX dust poof — it has a face and solid body mass, so players may interpret it as a new NPC or enemy instead of a momentary effect; (2) the asset overlaps/occludes the existing dark entity beneath it, creating a brief moment of visual noise where two distinct things occupy the same tile. No tonal clash with the background, no outline disappearance, and no HUD confusion observed.
